---
title: "Diego Carvalho"
image: media/dc-01.jpg
toc: false
about:
  template: jolla
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 12em
  links:
    - text: "{{< ai google-scholar >}} Google Scholar"
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=guvvdjEAAAAJ
      target: _blank
    - text: "{{< ai orcid >}} ORCID"
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0003-1592-6327
      target: _blank
    - text: "{{< ai scopus >}} Scopus"
      href: http://www.scopus.com/inward/authorDetails.url?authorID=56548303600
      target: _blank
    - text: "{{< ai clarivate >}} Web of Science"
      href: http://www.researcherid.com/rid/E-2722-2013
      target: _blank
    - text: "{{< ai sciprofiles >}} SciProfiles"
      href: https://sciprofiles.com/profile/667510
      target: _blank
---

Diego Carvalho is a tenured Professor in the Department of Higher Education at Cefet/RJ, where he teaches in the Production Engineering Course. Holding both a Bachelor's degree (UFRJ's Polytechnic School) and a Master's and a Doctorate in Systems Engineering and Computer Science from PESC/COPPE/UFRJ, he has demonstrated a consistent ability to connect research with real-world impact. His extensive work includes research at CERN's DELPHI Experiment, participation in European Commission grid computing initiatives (such as EELA and GISELA), and various CNPq-supported software development projects. Beyond research, he has held several senior leadership roles at Cefet/RJ, notably serving as Head of the Production Engineering course, Head of the Graduate Program in Production and Systems Engineering, Head of the Higher Education Department, and Pro-Rector for both Budget & Management and Strategic Management. A specialist in distributed systems, parallel architectures, grid technologies, data mining, and big data, he has contributions recognized through his Senior Membership in both the IEEE and the Internet Society.
